title: Braised Mexican Goat Meat Tacos
description: Tender goat meat, slow-cooked in a rich, spicy sauce, served in a crispy taco shell, a classic Jalisco-style dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 275°F (135°C).
In a large Dutch oven, heat the lard or oil over medium-high heat.
Add the goat meat and cook until browned on all sides, 5-7 minutes.
Add the ancho chili powder, cumin, coriander, paprika, and chipotle peppers. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and braise the goat meat for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
While the goat meat is cooking, toast the tortillas by wrapping them in foil and heating them in the oven for 10 minutes.
Once the goat meat is cooked, shred it with two forks and stir in the tomato paste.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the goat meat mixture onto a tortilla and topping with shredded cheese, a dollop of sour cream, and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Serve with your favorite taco toppings, such as diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, or diced avocado.
Make sure to serve with a side of warm tortilla chips or tostadas, for a crunchy snack.
If you want to make this recipe more substantial, serve with a side of Mexican street corn or a simple green salad.
For a spicy kick, add diced jalapeños or serrano peppers to the goat meat mixture.
For a vegetarian version, substitute the goat meat with roasted portobello mushrooms or sautéed spinach.
To make this recipe ahead of time, braise the goat meat a day in advance, then reheat it before assembling the tacos.
To make this recipe gluten-free, substitute the all-purpose flour with gluten-free flour or omit the flour altogether.
To make this recipe vegan, substitute the lard or oil with vegan oil and omit the cheese and sour cream.
For a more intense flavor, use beef broth instead of chicken broth.
To make this recipe in a slow cooker, brown the goat meat and cook the sauce in a skillet, then transfer everything to the slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
